element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Choose from 8 NFL teams (Los Angeles, Denver, San Francisco, Washington, Miami, New York, Chicago, Houston)
Select offensive/defensive plays; optional play card for 4-player mode to hide selections from opponents
Isometric perspective with 3D appearance; manages multiple moving objects (all players) simultaneously on screen
Digitized voices announce 'Ready! Set! Hut, hut..', 'Touchdown!', 'First down!' - primitive speech synthesis
Comprehensive position-specific depth charts with actual 1989 NFL season player stats, numbers, and face graphics
Nintendo Power mascot announces final result (win/loss) post-game

typical_tension_triggers: Play selection phase (especially with opponent visibility concerns in multiplayer)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based football rhythm with play selection tension followed by real-time execution resolution

difficulty_curve.early_game: Team and play selection learning curve
notable_spikes: Play calling complexity increases with AI opponent unpredictability

notable_level_patterns.description: Each game follows standard football format: team selection → play-by-play selection and execution → scoring resolution → Nester commentary
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Standard single or multiplayer game match

notable_level_patterns.description: Play card allows teammates to select plays without opponents seeing; requires physical card and on-screen input obfuscation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: 4-player game with NES Four Score adapter

notable_level_patterns.description: Players manage depth chart positions with actual NFL player stats from 1989 season; affects play outcomes

sources_used: Wikipedia - NES Play Action Football (Overview section on gameplay mechanics, isometric view, digitized voices, Nester appearance, team selection)
The Cutting Room Floor - NES Play Action Football (Technical features, debugging material, Virtual Console changes)
